general: Welcome to Last Orders. The floor's sticky, the ale's flat, and I've seen better crowds at a funeral.
You break it, you bought it. You bought it, I keep it anyway.
The dartboard's crooked, the beams are rotten, and so am I. Drink up.
I've watered down the ale, but don't worry — the water's also ale.
We don't do cocktails. We do consequences.
That stool's reserved for the ghost of the last smart-mouth. Sit careful.
You lot look like a quest gone wrong.
House rule: no prophecies at the bar. Last one caused a stampede.
The soup of the day is ale. The soup of every day is ale.
If the neon sign flickers, that's not an omen. Probably.
I've thrown out kings, dragons, and one very persistent mime. Don't test me.
Tips go in the jar. Complaints go in the cellar. Nobody comes back from the cellar.
Enjoy yourselves. It unsettles me, but enjoy yourselves.
roundStart: Right, listen up you lot, and pretend you have brains.
Next round's starting. Try not to embarrass your ancestors.
Sharpen up. This one separates the patrons from the puddles.
Places, everyone. And by places I mean sit down and shut it.
Another round, another chance to disappoint me personally.
Eyes front. The quicker you play, the quicker I can close.
playerDid: I've seen drunker people answer faster.
Somewhere, a village is missing its genius. Not here though.
That answer's going on the wall of shame, right next to the dart hole.
Not bad. For someone drinking on credit.
The pub cat could've done better, and the pub cat is dead.
scores: Here's the Bar Tab. Settle up in points or shame — your choice.
The chalkboard doesn't lie. Unlike most of you.
Look at those scores. My condolences to the bottom half.
Winners drink free. We don't have winners here, so pay up.
The leaderboard's been updated. The cellar's been unlocked. Motivation, that is.
Half of you owe me money. All of you owe me an apology.
death: Another one for the cellar. Mind the stairs — not that it matters now.
We lost one. Round of silence. ...Right, that's enough.
They died as they lived: getting easy questions wrong.
Don't worry, ghosts drink half price.
victory: A winner. In THIS pub. The neon sign just flickered in disbelief.
Congratulations. Your prize is telling everyone about it forever.
Champion of Last Orders. That and two coppers gets you a flat ale.
You've won. Now get out — LAST ORDERS!
